data.narrative:
Quantinuum is a publicly traded quantum computing company founded by Ilyas Khan. The peer universe comprises five same-sector public-stage comparables: IonQ ($11.0B), Rigetti Computing ($5.5B), D-Wave Quantum ($2.0B), Infleqtion ($1.8B), and Archer Materials ($60M). This sample spans a wide valuation range reflecting heterogeneous stage maturity and commercialization progress within the sector.

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach given data availability. The median valuation across the five-company cohort is $2.0B, with a range from $60M to $11.0B. Quantinuum's current trading valuation of $10B sits materially above this median and near the high end of the peer distribution, reflecting its position as one of the sector's largest funded entities with $2.7B+ raised to date.

The analysis supports a triangulated range of $1.7B to $2.3B (median $2.0B), derived from peer-median methodology. Quantinuum's current $10B valuation represents a significant premium to peer median—approximately 5× the reference point. Key uncertainties limiting precision include the single-method triangulation (peer-median only), wide dispersion within the quantum computing comp set ($60M to $11.0B), and nascent revenue visibility across the sector. Any revaluation should account for company-specific metrics (revenue, burn rate, IP defensibility) not captured in peer-based frameworks.
